name: Carbonek
description: Carbonek is a mystical castle of immense importance in Arthurian legend, primarily associated with the Holy Grail. It is often depicted as a perilous and enchanted place, shrouded in mystery and guarded by strange forces. The castle is the home of the Fisher King, who suffers from a debilitating wound that renders him unable to move or fulfill his duties, causing the surrounding lands to become barren and desolate. Carbonek is also where the Holy Grail resides, often protected by a procession of figures carrying sacred objects. Knights who seek the Grail must prove their worthiness to even approach the castle, and many fail in their quest, succumbing to the trials and temptations within. The castle itself is often described as shifting and unreal, reflecting the spiritual state of those who seek it. It is a place of profound spiritual significance, representing both the potential for salvation and the dangers of worldly desire. The successful seeker, like Galahad, must be pure of heart and selfless in their intentions to achieve the Grail and heal the Fisher King, restoring prosperity to the land. Carbonek, therefore, is more than just a location; it's a symbol of spiritual quest, sacrifice, and the ultimate attainment of divine grace. The castle's atmosphere is often charged with an otherworldly aura, indicative of its connection to the divine and its separation from the mundane world. Its existence challenges the boundaries between reality and the supernatural, serving as a focal point for the Arthurian cycle's themes of faith, redemption, and the enduring power of the sacred.
subtitle: Castle of the Holy Grail

keyMyths.title: The Grail Quest
keyMyths.description: Knights, such as Percival and Galahad, seek the Holy Grail at Carbonek, facing trials and temptations to prove their worthiness. Only the purest knight can achieve the Grail and heal the Fisher King.

keyMyths.title: The Wounded Fisher King
keyMyths.description: The Fisher King resides in Carbonek, suffering from a wound that causes the land to become barren. His healing is tied to the attainment of the Holy Grail by a worthy knight.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: A foundational text for Arthurian legends, featuring Carbonek and the Grail quest.
sources.text: A foundational text for Arthurian legends, featuring Carbonek and the Grail quest.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.work: Perceval, the Story of the Grail
sources.description: An early Arthurian romance that introduces the Grail and the Fisher King.
sources.text: An early Arthurian romance that introduces the Grail and the Fisher King.

sources.author: Robert de Boron
sources.description: Connects the Holy Grail to Joseph of Arimathea and its journey to Britain.
